evidence: The hypothesis requires G to be triangle-free ('if G is triangle-free and path(G) <= 4').

evidence: path(G) here is defined as the size of a largest induced path in G (largestInducedPathSize).
verify_note: largestInducedPathSize is defined in-file as largest induced path; label exact

evidence: The conclusion is that G is well totally dominated, i.e. all minimal total dominating sets have the same size.
verify_note: conclusion IsWellTotallyDominated is a property of minimal total dominating sets; label exact

evidence: The hypothesis equates the independence number alpha(G) with the number of pendant vertices.
verify_note: hypothesis uses G.indepNum (independence number); label exact

evidence: P is the set of pendant vertices, defined as the vertices of degree 1.

evidence: The conclusion IsWellTotallyDominated concerns minimal total dominating sets all having the same size.

evidence: The hypothesis bounds the pendant count by deg_avg(G-complement), the average degree of the complement of G.

evidence: The statement compares the average degree of the complement with the number of degree-1 (pendant) vertices.

evidence: The conclusion is that G is well totally dominated, a property of its minimal total dominating sets.

evidence: The statement bounds path(G), the floor of the average distance, by dist_avg(A) plus half the average eccentricity of M.
verify_note: path(G)=floor avg distance and distavg both distance-based; label exact

evidence: ecc_avg(M) is the average eccentricity of the maximum-degree vertices.

evidence: A and M are the sets of minimum-degree and maximum-degree vertices of G.

evidence: l(v) is the independence number of the subgraph induced by the open neighborhood N(v).

evidence: The conclusion is that G is well totally dominated, a condition on its minimal total dominating sets.

evidence: The statement relates the domination number gamma(G), the independent domination number, and well total domination.

evidence: gamma_i(G) is the independent domination number, the minimum size of an independent dominating set.
verify_note: indepDominationNumber = min independent dominating set size; label exact

evidence: The statement bounds path(G), the floor of the average distance, by twice the average distance from all vertices to M.
verify_note: path(G) and distavg G M are distance invariants; label exact

evidence: M is the set of maximum-degree vertices of G.

evidence: The statement bounds path(G), the floor of the average distance, by dist_avg(C,V) + dist_avg(M,V).
verify_note: path(G) and distavg over C,M are distance invariants; label exact

evidence: C is the set of center vertices, those with minimum eccentricity.
verify_note: C = center G, the min-eccentricity vertices; label exact

evidence: M is the set of maximum-degree vertices of G.

evidence: path(G) is the floor of the average distance and dp(G) counts pairs of vertices at distance diam(G).

evidence: The bound is 2*rad(G)/dp(G), where rad(G) is the radius of G.
